title: Giving Up Wrong View
text:
## 16. The End of Relishing

Then a mendicant went up to the Buddha … and said to him: “Sir, how does one know and see so that wrong view is given up?”

“Mendicant, knowing and seeing the eye, sights, eye consciousness, and eye contact as impermanent, wrong view is given up. … And also knowing and seeing the pleasant, painful, or neutral feeling that arises dependent on mind contact as impermanent, wrong view is given up. This is how to know and see so that wrong view is given up.”
